channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. Lett.
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review Letters
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2026 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Emergent Topology from Landau Level Mixing in Quantum Hall-Superconductor Nanostructures
item.description:
Author(s): Yuriko Baba, Alfredo Levy Yeyati, and Pablo Burset

We demonstrate the emergence of novel topological phases in quantum Hall-superconductor hybrid structures driven by Landau-level mixing and spin-orbit coupling. For a narrow superconducting stripe atop a two-dimensional electron gas, hybridization of chiral Andreev edge states yields a rich phase di…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176601] Published Mon Apr 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yuriko Baba, Alfredo Levy Yeyati, and Pablo Burset

We demonstrate the emergence of novel topological phases in quantum Hall-superconductor hybrid structures driven by Landau-level mixing and spin-orbit coupling. For a narrow superconducting stripe atop a two-dimensional electron gas, hybridization of chiral Andreev edge states yields a rich phase di…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176601] Published Mon Apr 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Emergent Topology from Landau Level Mixing in Quantum Hall-Superconductor Nanostructures
item.dc:creator: Yuriko Baba, Alfredo Levy Yeyati, and Pablo Burset
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials
item.description:

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Yuriko Baba, Alfredo Levy Yeyati, and Pablo Burset

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory on emergent topological phases in quantum Hall-superconductor heterostructures, relevant for topological materials.

Tags: #topological #superconductor #quantum_Hall

Abstract: Author(s): Yuriko Baba, Alfredo Levy Yeyati, and Pablo Burset We demonstrate the emergence of novel topological phases in quantum Hall-superconductor hybrid structures driven by Landau-level mixing and spin-orbit coupling. For a narrow superconducting stripe atop a two-dimensional electron gas, hybridization of chiral Andreev edge states yields a rich phase di… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176601] Published Mon Apr 27, 2026

item.title: Elastic Response and Instabilities of Anomalous Hall Crystals
item.description:
Author(s): Félix Desrochers, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, Joe Huxford, Adarsh S. Patri, T. Senthil, and Yong Baek Kim

Anomalous Hall crystals (AHCs) are exotic phases of matter that simultaneously break continuous translation symmetry and exhibit the quantum anomalous Hall effect. AHCs have recently been proposed to explain the observation of an integer quantum anomalous Hall phase in a multilayer graphene system. …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166503] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Félix Desrochers, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, Joe Huxford, Adarsh S. Patri, T. Senthil, and Yong Baek Kim

Anomalous Hall crystals (AHCs) are exotic phases of matter that simultaneously break continuous translation symmetry and exhibit the quantum anomalous Hall effect. AHCs have recently been proposed to explain the observation of an integer quantum anomalous Hall phase in a multilayer graphene system. …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166503] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Elastic Response and Instabilities of Anomalous Hall Crystals
item.dc:creator: Félix Desrochers, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, Joe Huxford, Adarsh S. Patri, T. Senthil, and Yong Baek Kim
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials
item.description:
Authors: Félix Desrochers, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, Joe Huxford, Adarsh S. Patri, T. Senthil, and Yong Baek Kim

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Félix Desrochers, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, Joe Huxford, Adarsh S. Patri, T. Senthil, and Yong Baek Kim

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory on Anomalous Hall Crystals and quantum anomalous Hall effect in multilayer graphene, highly relevant.

Tags: #anomalous_Hall #quantum_Hall #graphene #topological_materials

Abstract: Author(s): Félix Desrochers, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, Joe Huxford, Adarsh S. Patri, T. Senthil, and Yong Baek Kim Anomalous Hall crystals (AHCs) are exotic phases of matter that simultaneously break continuous translation symmetry and exhibit the quantum anomalous Hall effect. AHCs have recently been proposed to explain the observation of an integer quantum anomalous Hall phase in a multilayer graphene system. … [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166503] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026

item.title: Ultrafast Moiré-Resolved Spectroscopy of Interlayer-Exciton Thermalization in Twisted ${\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}/{\mathrm{WS}}_{2}$ Heterobilayers
item.description:
Author(s): Jinjae Kim, Hyeonho Lee, Claudia Gollner, Yannick Pleimling, Sumin Lee, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Moon-Ho Jo, Tony Heinz, and Hyunyong Choi

Interlayer excitons (IXs) in transition metal dichalcogenide moiré heterobilayers offer a versatile platform for exploring strongly correlated quantum phases. Despite recent progress in probing excitonic properties, the thermalization dynamics of IXs, particularly within moiré superlattices, remain …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166902] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jinjae Kim, Hyeonho Lee, Claudia Gollner, Yannick Pleimling, Sumin Lee, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Moon-Ho Jo, Tony Heinz, and Hyunyong Choi

Interlayer excitons (IXs) in transition metal dichalcogenide moiré heterobilayers offer a versatile platform for exploring strongly correlated quantum phases. Despite recent progress in probing excitonic properties, the thermalization dynamics of IXs, particularly within moiré superlattices, remain …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166902] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Ultrafast Moiré-Resolved Spectroscopy of Interlayer-Exciton Thermalization in Twisted ${\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}/{\mathrm{WS}}_{2}$ Heterobilayers
item.dc:creator: Jinjae Kim, Hyeonho Lee, Claudia Gollner, Yannick Pleimling, Sumin Lee, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Moon-Ho Jo, Tony Heinz, and Hyunyong Choi

Despite recent progress in probing excitonic properties, the thermalization dynamics of IXs, particularly within moiré superlattices, remain … [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166902] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026

item.title: Emergent Chirality and Enantiomeric Selectivity in Layered ${\mathrm{NbOX}}_{2}$ Crystals
item.description:
Author(s): Martin Gutierrez-Amigo, Claudia Felser, Ion Errea, and Maia G. Vergniory

Theorists have identified a phase that could facilitate the switching of a crystal between its right-handed and left-handed versions.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166605] Published Thu Apr 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Martin Gutierrez-Amigo, Claudia Felser, Ion Errea, and Maia G. Vergniory

Theorists have identified a phase that could facilitate the switching of a crystal between its right-handed and left-handed versions.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166605] Published Thu Apr 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Emergent Chirality and Enantiomeric Selectivity in Layered ${\mathrm{NbOX}}_{2}$ Crystals
item.dc:creator: Martin Gutierrez-Amigo, Claudia Felser, Ion Errea, and Maia G. Vergniory
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Martin Gutierrez-Amigo, Claudia Felser, Ion Errea, and Maia G. Vergniory

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory on emergent chirality in layered crystals, relevant to materials physics.

Tags: #materials #chirality #layered_materials

Abstract: Author(s): Martin Gutierrez-Amigo, Claudia Felser, Ion Errea, and Maia G. Vergniory Theorists have identified a phase that could facilitate the switching of a crystal between its right-handed and left-handed versions. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166605] Published Thu Apr 23, 2026

item.title: Orbital Magnetization of Correlated States in Twisted Bilayer Transition Metal Dichalcogenides
item.description:
Author(s): Xiaoyu Liu, Chong Wang, Haoran Chen, Xiao-Wei Zhang, Ting Cao, and Di Xiao

The modern theory of orbital magnetization, derived for noninteracting electrons, applied to strongly correlated states remains valid for Hartree–Fock states, so can be used to interpret magnetization in interacting moiré systems.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166606] Published Thu Apr 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiaoyu Liu, Chong Wang, Haoran Chen, Xiao-Wei Zhang, Ting Cao, and Di Xiao

The modern theory of orbital magnetization, derived for noninteracting electrons, applied to strongly correlated states remains valid for Hartree–Fock states, so can be used to interpret magnetization in interacting moiré systems.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166606] Published Thu Apr 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Orbital Magnetization of Correlated States in Twisted Bilayer Transition Metal Dichalcogenides
item.dc:creator: Xiaoyu Liu, Chong Wang, Haoran Chen, Xiao-Wei Zhang, Ting Cao, and Di Xiao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

Abstract: Author(s): Xiaoyu Liu, Chong Wang, Haoran Chen, Xiao-Wei Zhang, Ting Cao, and Di Xiao The modern theory of orbital magnetization, derived for noninteracting electrons, applied to strongly correlated states remains valid for Hartree–Fock states, so can be used to interpret magnetization in interacting moiré systems. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166606] Published Thu Apr 23, 2026

item.title: Self-Induced Floquet States via Three-Wave Processes in Synthetic Antiferromagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Thibaut Devolder and Joo-Von Kim

We present a mechanism for self-induced Floquet states involving acoustic and optical modes in synthetic antiferromagnets. By driving optical modes off-resonantly with radio frequency fields in the canted antiferromagnetic state, limit cycles arising from the predator-prey dynamics of the acoustic a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166702] Published Thu Apr 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Thibaut Devolder and Joo-Von Kim

We present a mechanism for self-induced Floquet states involving acoustic and optical modes in synthetic antiferromagnets. By driving optical modes off-resonantly with radio frequency fields in the canted antiferromagnetic state, limit cycles arising from the predator-prey dynamics of the acoustic a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166702] Published Thu Apr 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Self-Induced Floquet States via Three-Wave Processes in Synthetic Antiferromagnets
item.dc:creator: Thibaut Devolder and Joo-Von Kim
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

By driving optical modes off-resonantly with radio frequency fields in the canted antiferromagnetic state, limit cycles arising from the predator-prey dynamics of the acoustic a… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166702] Published Thu Apr 23, 2026

item.title: Novel Quantum Spin Liquid States in the $S=\frac{1}{2}$ Three-Dimensional Compound ${\mathrm{Y}}_{3}{\mathrm{Cu}}_{2}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{3}{\mathrm{O}}_{14}$
item.description:
Author(s): Saikat Nandi, Rounak Das, M. Hemmida, M. U. Akbar, H.-A. Krug von Nidda, Jörg Sichelschmidt, Sagar Mahapatra, Marlis Schuller, N. Büttgen, J. M. Wilkinson, M. P. Saravanan, Indra Dasgupta, and A. V. Mahajan

The three-dimensional $S=\frac{1}{2}$ system ${\mathrm{Y}}_{3}{\mathrm{Cu}}_{2}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{3}{\mathrm{O}}_{14}$ consists of two inequivalent ${\mathrm{Cu}}^{2+}$ sites, each forming an edge shared triangular lattice. Our magnetic susceptibility $χ(T)$, specific heat ${C}_{p}(T)$, $^{89}\mathrm{Y}…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166502] Published Wed Apr 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Saikat Nandi, Rounak Das, M. Hemmida, M. U. Akbar, H.-A. Krug von Nidda, Jörg Sichelschmidt, Sagar Mahapatra, Marlis Schuller, N. Büttgen, J. M. Wilkinson, M. P. Saravanan, Indra Dasgupta, and A. V. Mahajan

The three-dimensional S=12 system Y3Cu2Sb3O14 consists of two inequivalent Cu2+ sites, each forming an edge shared triangular lattice. Our magnetic susceptibility χ(T), specific heat Cp(T), Y89 nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR), muon spin relaxation (μSR), and electron spin resonance (ESR) measuremen…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166502] Published Wed Apr 22, 2026
item.dc:title: Novel Quantum Spin Liquid States in the $S=\frac{1}{2}$ Three-Dimensional Compound ${\mathrm{Y}}_{3}{\mathrm{Cu}}_{2}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{3}{\mathrm{O}}_{14}$

Saravanan, Indra Dasgupta, and A. V. Mahajan The three-dimensional $S=\frac{1}{2}$ system ${\mathrm{Y}}_{3}{\mathrm{Cu}}_{2}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{3}{\mathrm{O}}_{14}$ consists of two inequivalent ${\mathrm{Cu}}^{2+}$ sites, each forming an edge shared triangular lattice. Our magnetic susceptibility $χ(T)$, specific heat ${C}_{p}(T)$, $^{89}\mathrm{Y}… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166502] Published Wed Apr 22, 2026

item.title: Quantum Transport Spectroscopy of Pseudomagnetic Field in Graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Divya Sahani, Sunit Das, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Amit Agarwal, and Aveek Bid

A quantitative detection of strain-induced pseudomagnetic fields in graphene resolves a challenge in Dirac materials by showing that valley-antisymmetric gauge fields imprint a universal, symmetry-protected beating signature on the Shubnikov-de Haas oscillations in the magnetotransport.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166604] Published Wed Apr 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Divya Sahani, Sunit Das, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Amit Agarwal, and Aveek Bid

A quantitative detection of strain-induced pseudomagnetic fields in graphene resolves a challenge in Dirac materials by showing that valley-antisymmetric gauge fields imprint a universal, symmetry-protected beating signature on the Shubnikov-de Haas oscillations in the magnetotransport.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166604] Published Wed Apr 22, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum Transport Spectroscopy of Pseudomagnetic Field in Graphene
item.dc:creator: Divya Sahani, Sunit Das, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Amit Agarwal, and Aveek Bid
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials
item.description:

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental quantum transport spectroscopy of pseudomagnetic fields in graphene; highly relevant to Dirac materials.

Tags: #graphene #Dirac_semimetal #transport #magnetotransport #pseudomagnetic_field

Abstract: Author(s): Divya Sahani, Sunit Das, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Amit Agarwal, and Aveek Bid A quantitative detection of strain-induced pseudomagnetic fields in graphene resolves a challenge in Dirac materials by showing that valley-antisymmetric gauge fields imprint a universal, symmetry-protected beating signature on the Shubnikov-de Haas oscillations in the magnetotransport. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166604] Published Wed Apr 22, 2026

item.title: Floquet Spin Splitting and Spin Generation in Antiferromagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Bo Li, Ding-Fu Shao, and Alexey A. Kovalev

Polarized light lifts spin degeneracy in conventional antiferromagnets without requiring spin-orbit coupling and generates steady-state pure spin current.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166701] Published Wed Apr 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bo Li, Ding-Fu Shao, and Alexey A. Kovalev

Polarized light lifts spin degeneracy in conventional antiferromagnets without requiring spin-orbit coupling and generates steady-state pure spin current.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166701] Published Wed Apr 22, 2026
item.dc:title: Floquet Spin Splitting and Spin Generation in Antiferromagnets

Abstract: Author(s): Bo Li, Ding-Fu Shao, and Alexey A. Kovalev Polarized light lifts spin degeneracy in conventional antiferromagnets without requiring spin-orbit coupling and generates steady-state pure spin current. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166701] Published Wed Apr 22, 2026

item.title: Lattice Excitations with Finite Polarization and Magnetization
item.description:
Author(s): Mike Pols, Carl P. Romao, and Dominik M. Juraschek

Ferrons are a type of quasiparticle corresponding to elementary excitations of the ferroelectric order. Analogously to how magnons modulate and transport magnetization, ferrons modulate and transport electric polarization. Here, we introduce multiferrons as elementary excitations with both electric …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166801] Published Wed Apr 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mike Pols, Carl P. Romao, and Dominik M. Juraschek

Ferrons are a type of quasiparticle corresponding to elementary excitations of the ferroelectric order. Analogously to how magnons modulate and transport magnetization, ferrons modulate and transport electric polarization. Here, we introduce multiferrons as elementary excitations with both electric …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166801] Published Wed Apr 22, 2026
item.dc:title: Lattice Excitations with Finite Polarization and Magnetization
item.dc:creator: Mike Pols, Carl P. Romao, and Dominik M. Juraschek

Abstract: Author(s): Mike Pols, Carl P. Romao, and Dominik M. Juraschek Ferrons are a type of quasiparticle corresponding to elementary excitations of the ferroelectric order. Analogously to how magnons modulate and transport magnetization, ferrons modulate and transport electric polarization. Here, we introduce multiferrons as elementary excitations with both electric … [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166801] Published Wed Apr 22, 2026

item.title: Spectral Decomposition and High-Accuracy Green’s Functions: Overcoming the Nyquist-Shannon Limit via Complex-time Krylov Expansion
item.description:
Author(s): S. Paeckel

The accurate computation of low-energy spectra of strongly correlated quantum many-body systems, typically accessed via Green’s functions, is a long-standing problem posing enormous challenges to numerical methods. When the spectral decomposition is obtained from Fourier transforming a time series, …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 160401] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): S. Paeckel

The accurate computation of low-energy spectra of strongly correlated quantum many-body systems, typically accessed via Green’s functions, is a long-standing problem posing enormous challenges to numerical methods. When the spectral decomposition is obtained from Fourier transforming a time series, …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 160401] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Spectral Decomposition and High-Accuracy Green’s Functions: Overcoming the Nyquist-Shannon Limit via Complex-time Krylov Expansion

Paeckel The accurate computation of low-energy spectra of strongly correlated quantum many-body systems, typically accessed via Green’s functions, is a long-standing problem posing enormous challenges to numerical methods. When the spectral decomposition is obtained from Fourier transforming a time series, … [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 160401] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026

item.title: Racetrack Computing with a Topological Boundary Ratchet
item.description:
Author(s): Parisa Omidvar, Markus Bestler, Sima Zahedi Fard, Oded Zilberberg, and Marc Serra-Garcia

Multistable order parameters provide a natural means of encoding nonvolatile information in spatial domains, a concept that forms the foundation of magnetic memory devices. However, this stability inherently conflicts with the need to move information around the device for processing and readout. Wh…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166601] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Parisa Omidvar, Markus Bestler, Sima Zahedi Fard, Oded Zilberberg, and Marc Serra-Garcia

Multistable order parameters provide a natural means of encoding nonvolatile information in spatial domains, a concept that forms the foundation of magnetic memory devices. However, this stability inherently conflicts with the need to move information around the device for processing and readout. Wh…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166601] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Racetrack Computing with a Topological Boundary Ratchet
item.dc:creator: Parisa Omidvar, Markus Bestler, Sima Zahedi Fard, Oded Zilberberg, and Marc Serra-Garcia

However, this stability inherently conflicts with the need to move information around the device for processing and readout. Wh… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166601] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026

item.title: Compatible Instability: Gauge Constraints of Elasticity Inherited by Electronic Nematic Criticality
item.description:
Author(s): W. Joe Meese and Rafael M. Fernandes

A 19th-century theory of elasticity inspires a new way to analyze a quantum phase transition that has become central to modern quantum materials research.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166501] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): W. Joe Meese and Rafael M. Fernandes

A 19th-century theory of elasticity inspires a new way to analyze a quantum phase transition that has become central to modern quantum materials research.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166501] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Compatible Instability: Gauge Constraints of Elasticity Inherited by Electronic Nematic Criticality
item.dc:creator: W. Joe Meese and Rafael M. Fernandes

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials
item.description:
Authors: W. Joe Meese and Rafael M. Fernandes

Last/corresponding-author proxy: W. Joe Meese and Rafael M. Fernandes

Score 10/10 A_MUST_READ

Why: Direct hit: theory on electronic nematic criticality, a core interest for quantum materials research.

Tags: #nematicity #quantum_materials #quantum_phase_transition #theory

Abstract: Author(s): W. Joe Meese and Rafael M. Fernandes A 19th-century theory of elasticity inspires a new way to analyze a quantum phase transition that has become central to modern quantum materials research. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166501] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026

item.title: Terahertz-Driven Parametric Excitation of Raman-Active Phonons in ${\mathrm{LaAlO}}_{3}$
item.description:
Author(s): M. Basini, V. Unikandanunni, F. Gabriele, M. Cross, A. M. Derrico, A. X. Gray, M. C. Hoffmann, F. Forte, M. Cuoco, and S. Bonetti

Achieving parametric excitation in an oscillating physical system involves periodically adjusting one of its parameters to modulate the oscillator’s natural frequency. This phenomenon has been observed in numerous systems within physics and engineering, profoundly transforming modern science and tec…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 156902] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): M. Basini, V. Unikandanunni, F. Gabriele, M. Cross, A. M. Derrico, A. X. Gray, M. C. Hoffmann, F. Forte, M. Cuoco, and S. Bonetti

Achieving parametric excitation in an oscillating physical system involves periodically adjusting one of its parameters to modulate the oscillator’s natural frequency. This phenomenon has been observed in numerous systems within physics and engineering, profoundly transforming modern science and tec…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 156902] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026

item.title: False Vacuum Decay in Flat-Band Ferromagnets: Role of Quantum Geometry and Chiral Edge States
item.description:
Author(s): Fabian Pichler, Clemens Kuhlenkamp, and Michael Knap

Dynamical control of quantum matter is a challenging, yet promising direction for probing strongly correlated states. Motivated by recent experiments in twisted ${\mathrm{MoTe}}_{2}$ that demonstrated optical control of magnetization, we propose a protocol for probing magnetization dynamics in flat-…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 156502] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Fabian Pichler, Clemens Kuhlenkamp, and Michael Knap

Dynamical control of quantum matter is a challenging, yet promising direction for probing strongly correlated states. Motivated by recent experiments in twisted MoTe2 that demonstrated optical control of magnetization, we propose a protocol for probing magnetization dynamics in flat-band ferromagnet…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 156502] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026

item.title: Interlayer Charge-Density-Wave Vector Phase Induced Structural Chirality
item.description:
Author(s): Sen Shao, Wei-Chi Chiu, Tao Hou, Naizhou Wang, Ilya Belopolski, Yilin Zhao, Jinyang Ni, Qi Zhang, Yongkai Li, Jinjin Liu, Mohammad Yahyavi, Yuanjun Jin, Qiange Feng, Peiyuan Cui, Cheng-Long Zhang, Yugui Yao, Zhiwei Wang, Jia-Xin Yin, Su-Yang Xu, Qiong Ma, Wei-bo Gao, Md Shafayat Hossain, Arun Bansil, and Guoqing Chang

Chiral charge density waves (CDWs) have attracted intense interest due to their exotic quantum properties, yet the microscopic origin of structural chirality emerging from correlated charge order remains elusive. Here, we reveal that the interlayer phases of CDW vectors, an overlooked degree of free…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 156101] Published Tue Apr 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sen Shao, Wei-Chi Chiu, Tao Hou, Naizhou Wang, Ilya Belopolski, Yilin Zhao, Jinyang Ni, Qi Zhang, Yongkai Li, Jinjin Liu, Mohammad Yahyavi, Yuanjun Jin, Qiange Feng, Peiyuan Cui, Cheng-Long Zhang, Yugui Yao, Zhiwei Wang, Jia-Xin Yin, Su-Yang Xu, Qiong Ma, Wei-bo Gao, Md Shafayat Hossain, Arun Bansil, and Guoqing Chang

Chiral charge density waves (CDWs) have attracted intense interest due to their exotic quantum properties, yet the microscopic origin of structural chirality emerging from correlated charge order remains elusive. Here, we reveal that the interlayer phases of CDW vectors, an overlooked degree of free…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 156101] Published Tue Apr 14, 2026

Here, we reveal that the interlayer phases of CDW vectors, an overlooked degree of free… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 156101] Published Tue Apr 14, 2026

item.title: Momentum-Resolved Spectroscopy of Superconductivity with the Quantum Twisting Microscope
item.description:
Author(s): Yuval Waschitz, Ady Stern, and Yuval Oreg

By conserving in-plane momentum, the quantum twisting microscope directly measures Bogoliubov coherence factors and pairing amplitudes with full momentum resolution, providing information inaccessible to existing probes.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 156501] Published Tue Apr 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yuval Waschitz, Ady Stern, and Yuval Oreg

By conserving in-plane momentum, the quantum twisting microscope directly measures Bogoliubov coherence factors and pairing amplitudes with full momentum resolution, providing information inaccessible to existing probes.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 156501] Published Tue Apr 14, 2026
item.dc:title: Momentum-Resolved Spectroscopy of Superconductivity with the Quantum Twisting Microscope
item.dc:creator: Yuval Waschitz, Ady Stern, and Yuval Oreg

item.title: Non-Abelian Chern Band in Rhombohedral Graphene Multilayers
item.description:
Author(s): Taketo Uchida, Takuto Kawakami, and Mikito Koshino

Self-consistent Hartree–Fock calculations show that electron interactions spontaneously generate a doubly degenerate Chern band with non-Abelian Berry curvature driven by a Fock-term–induced nonsymmorphic symmetry.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 156602] Published Tue Apr 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Taketo Uchida, Takuto Kawakami, and Mikito Koshino

Self-consistent Hartree–Fock calculations show that electron interactions spontaneously generate a doubly degenerate Chern band with non-Abelian Berry curvature driven by a Fock-term–induced nonsymmorphic symmetry.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 156602] Published Tue Apr 14, 2026
item.dc:title: Non-Abelian Chern Band in Rhombohedral Graphene Multilayers
item.dc:creator: Taketo Uchida, Takuto Kawakami, and Mikito Koshino

item.title: Imaging Kekulé Spiral Order in Graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Can Zhang, Hua Chen, Ying Su, Fudi Zhou, Lili Zhou, Zhaoteng Dong, Mengya Ren, Lijun Zhang, Yu Zhang, and Yeliang Wang

The first experimental evidence that a 1D nanostructure induces Kekulé spiral order in graphene.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 156401] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Can Zhang, Hua Chen, Ying Su, Fudi Zhou, Lili Zhou, Zhaoteng Dong, Mengya Ren, Lijun Zhang, Yu Zhang, and Yeliang Wang

The first experimental evidence that a 1D nanostructure induces Kekulé spiral order in graphene.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 156401] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026

item.title: Anomalous Terahertz Nonlinearity in Disordered $s$-Wave Superconductor Close to the Superconductor-Insulator Transition
item.description:
Author(s): Hao Wang, Jiayu Yuan, Hongkai Shi, Haojie Li, Xiaoqing Jia, Xiaohui Song, Liyu Shi, Tianyi Wu, Li Yue, Yangmu Li, Kui Jin, Dong Wu, Jianlin Luo, Xinbo Wang, Tao Dong, and Nan-Lin Wang

A persistent normal-state third-harmonic generation (THG) signal extending up to ten times Tc and a multipeak THG spectrum in the superconducting state reveal an unexpected interplay between disorder, electronic correlations, and superconducting inhomogeneity.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146004] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hao Wang, Jiayu Yuan, Hongkai Shi, Haojie Li, Xiaoqing Jia, Xiaohui Song, Liyu Shi, Tianyi Wu, Li Yue, Yangmu Li, Kui Jin, Dong Wu, Jianlin Luo, Xinbo Wang, Tao Dong, and Nan-Lin Wang

A persistent normal-state third-harmonic generation (THG) signal extending up to ten times Tc and a multipeak THG spectrum in the superconducting state reveal an unexpected interplay between disorder, electronic correlations, and superconducting inhomogeneity.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146004] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026

item.title: Antiferromagnetic Quantum Critical Point and Superconductivity in Self-Intercalated TMD ${\mathrm{V}}_{1/4}{\mathrm{VS}}_{2}$ under High Pressure
item.description:
Author(s): Jingwei Miao, Jiajia Feng, Wei Zhong, Jianing Xu, Naohisa Hirao, Hirokazu Kadobayashi, Sheng Jiang, Fang Chen, Bin Chen, and Binbin Yue

Self-intercalation in transition metal dichalcogenides (TMDs) offers a unique strategy for doping and spin ordering that preserves structural integrity, minimizing lattice distortions. This modification introduces additional electron density, spin states, and potentially spontaneous superlattice for…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146501] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026

Author(s): Jingwei Miao, Jiajia Feng, Wei Zhong, Jianing Xu, Naohisa Hirao, Hirokazu Kadobayashi, Sheng Jiang, Fang Chen, Bin Chen, and Binbin Yue

This modification introduces additional electron density, spin states, and potentially spontaneous superlattice for…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146501] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026

item.title: Altermagnetic Polarons: The Fate of Altermagnetic Band Splittings at Strong Coupling
item.description:
Author(s): Maria Daghofer, Krzysztof Wohlfeld, and Jeroen van den Brink

While a spin-dependent band splitting is one of the characteristic features of altermagnets, the conventional band picture itself breaks down in the many altermagnets that are correlated Mott materials. We employ two numerical many-body methods, the self-consistent Born approximation and a variation…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146502] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Maria Daghofer, Krzysztof Wohlfeld, and Jeroen van den Brink

While a spin-dependent band splitting is one of the characteristic features of altermagnets, the conventional band picture itself breaks down in the many altermagnets that are correlated Mott materials. We employ two numerical many-body methods, the self-consistent Born approximation and a variation…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146502] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026

item.title: Microscopic Phase-Transition Theory of Charge Density Waves: Revealing Hidden Crossovers of Phason and Amplitudon
item.description:
Author(s): F. Yang and L. Q. Chen

We develop a self-consistent phase-transition theory of charge density waves (CDWs), starting from a purely microscopic model. Specifically, we derive a microscopic CDW gap equation $|{\mathrm{Δ}}_{0}(T)|$, taking into account the thermal phase fluctuations (i.e., thermal excitation of phason) and t…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146503] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): F. Yang and L. Q. Chen

We develop a self-consistent phase-transition theory of charge density waves (CDWs), starting from a purely microscopic model. Specifically, we derive a microscopic CDW gap equation |Δ0(T)|, taking into account the thermal phase fluctuations (i.e., thermal excitation of phason) and their influence o…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146503] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026

item.title: Dirac Node Pinning from Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya Interactions in a Kagome Spin Liquid
item.description:
Author(s): Ajesh Kumar, Byungmin Kang, and Patrick A. Lee

Recent experiments on the Kagome spin liquid candidate material ${\mathrm{YCu}}_{3}(\mathrm{OH}{)}_{6}{\mathrm{Br}}_{2}[{\mathrm{Br}}_{1−y}(\mathrm{OH}{)}_{y}]$ suggest the presence of Dirac fermionic spinons near the magnetization plateau at $1/9$. Theories suggest that the spinons are charge neutr…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146704] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ajesh Kumar, Byungmin Kang, and Patrick A. Lee

Recent experiments on the Kagome spin liquid candidate material YCu3(OH)6Br2[Br1−y(OH)y] suggest the presence of Dirac fermionic spinons near the magnetization plateau at 1/9. Theories suggest that the spinons are charge neutral spin-1/2 excitations, in a 2π/3 flux, which triples the unit cell. Gene…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146704] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Dirac Node Pinning from Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya Interactions in a Kagome Spin Liquid
item.dc:creator: Ajesh Kumar, Byungmin Kang, and Patrick A. Lee
